name: Niger

visa_free.notes: ECOWAS free movement protocol (in force since April 30, 1980). Niger withdrew from ECOWAS on January 29, 2025 but has confirmed ECOWAS nationals may continue to enter without a visa until further notice.
ECOWAS free movement protocol still in effect pending renegotiation. Also an AES (Alliance of Sahel States) co-member with Niger.
ECOWAS free movement protocol still in effect pending renegotiation.
Bilateral visa-free agreement. Chad nationals can enter Niger without a visa for up to 3 months.

Bilateral visa-free agreement. Moroccan nationals can enter Niger without a visa for up to 3 months.

visa_policy.notes: The official Niger e-visa portal is operated by Niger's Ministry of Interior and Decentralization, Directorate of Territorial Surveillance (DST), contact@dst.gov.ne; hosted on nigerevisaportal.midas-dev.com (IOM MIDAS system). The portal lists Tourist Visa (e-visa) as available for almost all nationalities except AFGHANISTAN (explicitly 'No eligible visas') and USA (banned from entry by Niger since December 2025). ECOWAS nationals (Benin, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, Cote d'Ivoire, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Mali, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Togo) retain visa-free entry for 90 days under the ECOWAS free movement protocol (in force since April 30, 1980), still applicable to Niger even after withdrawal effective January 29, 2025. Chad, Mauritania, Morocco, and Tunisia also have bilateral visa-free agreements with Niger for up to 90 days. Niger implemented a complete ban on US nationals in late December 2025 as reciprocal measure to the US travel ban; entry and transit is refused. The e-visa max stay is 30 days per aggregator sources (not explicitly stated on the portal). E-visa processing time per FAQ is 3-5 working days. Payment is in CFA francs. Only Tourist Visa type is offered via the portal.

entry_refused.notes: Entry and transit completely refused for US nationals as of December 2025. Niger announced a full ban on US nationals in retaliation to the US travel ban proclamation of December 16, 2025 (effective January 1, 2026). The ban is indefinite. US nationals are also refused in transit even if not leaving the aircraft.

Afghanistan nationals are explicitly listed as 'No eligible visas' on the Niger e-visa portal. Afghan nationals are not eligible for the e-visa and require a visa from a Nigerien diplomatic mission in advance. Transit is also refused.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Niger government source.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic passports (and service passports for public affairs) of China are exempt from visa requirements for stays up to 30 days. Agreement entered into force on December 15, 2018.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Turkey are exempt from visa requirements for Niger. Ordinary passport holders from Turkey require a visa.
Holders of diplomatic and service passports of Indonesia are exempt from visa requirements for Niger. Ordinary passport holders from Indonesia require a visa.

agreements: Alliance of Sahel States (AES) / Confederation of Sahel States — Niger is a founding member with Mali and Burkina Faso; common AES passport launched January 29, 2025; AES plans a common visa policy but no official framework published yet as of June 2026

ECOWAS free movement protocol (April 30, 1980) — Niger withdrew from ECOWAS January 29, 2025 but has confirmed ECOWAS nationals may continue to enter without a visa until further notice (transitional arrangement)

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Added 19 visa-free entries (15 ECOWAS nationals + Chad + Mauritania + Morocco + Tunisia) previously missing from visa_policy.visa_free — these were incorrectly listed as e-visa eligible instead.
CORRECTED: Added max_stay_days=30 to all e-visa entries (was null throughout) based on aggregator consensus; not explicitly stated on official portal.

CORRECTED: Removed United States from e-visa list and created entry_refused section — Niger imposed a complete, indefinite ban on US nationals in December 2025 (reciprocal to US travel ban; effective January 1, 2026). US nationals cannot enter or transit even if not leaving the aircraft.
CORRECTED: Removed non-country entries from e-visa list (Antarctica, Bouvet Island) which are not nationalities.
CORRECTED: Updated conditional_access eligible_nationalities from 'any' to specific ISO3 codes for China, Turkey, Indonesia diplomatic/service passport exemptions.
CORRECTED: Added Hong Kong (14 days) to visa_free from bilateral agreement.

NOTE: ECOWAS free movement entries are sourced from Wikipedia (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visa_policy_of_Niger), not a Niger government official page — marked source_official=false. No Niger official government website publishes this list.
gaps: Niger's Ministry of Interior website (interieur.gouv.ne) does not publish a visa policy or exemption list
Max stay days for e-visa not stated on the official portal itself; 30-day figure sourced from aggregators
E-visa fee amount not published on official portal (template shows placeholder values)

No official Niger government source (only Wikipedia/aggregators) confirms the visa-free bilateral agreements with Chad, Mauritania, Morocco, Tunisia and Hong Kong — recorded based on cross-source consensus but not primary official sources
AES common visa policy not formally published by Niger government as of June 2026
No official Niger Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (diplomatie.gouv.ne) publishes a full enumerated list of bilateral visa waiver agreements
No RBI or fast-track program details found on any official Niger source
No official source found for credential-based conditional access (e.g. US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, or residence permit holders being granted VoA or visa-free entry)

Additional diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreements reported by Wikipedia (Russia diplomatic+service, Italy diplomatic, UAE diplomatic, Cuba diplomatic) could not be confirmed from an official government source of either party — not recorded per official-source-first rule
Agreement dates and terms for Turkey and Indonesia diplomatic/service exemptions not published on the Niger Embassy Russia source
UAE nationals may have special visa-on-arrival access per Wikipedia; could not confirm from official Niger or UAE government source
Current operational status of US nationals in transit airports (all transit refused or only land borders?) not definitively confirmed from Niger government official source
ECOWAS transitional arrangement end date not formally announced; situation remains fluid as of June 2026

outbound_official.note: no official Niger government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Niger's Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (diplomatie.gouv.ne) has menu entries for 'Voyages à l'extérieur du Niger' and 'Guide du voyageur' but neither resolves to a working page (both link to '#'). No other official Niger government domain (gouv.ne, interieur.gouv.ne, diplomatie.gouv.ne) publishes an enumerated list of countries Niger ordinary-passport holders may enter without a prior consular visa.
